frameworks.name: predictive coding
frameworks.one_line: Bayesian framework where prediction errors from suppressed percept drive perceptual transitions

frameworks.name: mutual inhibition with adaptation
frameworks.one_line: Established bistability model based on competing populations and adaptation

frameworks.name: noise-driven bistability
frameworks.one_line: Model where stochastic noise alone triggers perceptual switches

frameworks.name: adaptation plus noise
frameworks.one_line: Hybrid model combining adaptation dynamics with noise-driven switching

core_question: Can a predictive coding model account for endogenous perceptual transitions during bistable vision and explain neural signals?
key_assumptions: perception follows a generic Bayesian predictive coding framework
endogenous transitions arise from residual evidence for the suppressed percept
prediction errors emerge from mismatch between predicted and inferred percept
manipulations: bistable ambiguous stimulus viewing during fMRI
model parameter encoding perceptual stabilization fitted per participant
voxel-wise comparison across competing bistability models
key_findings: simulations reproduced temporal characteristics of perceptual bistability
stabilization parameter correlated with measured transition frequency across participants
predictive coding prediction errors correlated with bilateral inferior frontal gyri and anterior insula signals
voxel-wise selection favored predictive coding over conventional analyses in frontal regions
interpretations: bistable perception can be cast as a consequence of predictive coding prediction errors
frontal prediction-error signals support a predictive coding account of perceptual switching
